title: SSRF in SendWebRequestBlock via IPv4-mapped IPv6 / CGNAT IP-validation bypass in _is_ip_blocked
description: _is_ip_blocked in autogpt_platform/backend/backend/util/request.py checks each resolved IP against BLOCKED_IP_NETWORKS but does not normalize IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g. ::ffff:169.254.169.254) to their IPv4 form before the check. Because Python's ipaddress module never treats an IPv6Address as 'in' an IPv4Network, an attacker-controlled hostname with an AAAA record pointing to an IPv4-mapped IPv6 representation of an internal/private IPv4 address bypasses the IPv4 blocklist entirely, letting SendWebRequestBlock reach internal services (cloud metadata, internal admin APIs). The blocklist also omitted the 100.64.0.0/10 (CGNAT, RFC 6598) range. Since resolution is hostname-based, no victim-side change is required.
